evals.prompt: I'm a new CPO at a 150-person B2B SaaS company. The product team has no real process, builds whatever sales asks, and has never done structured discovery. Where do I start? Which parts of the Modern Product Operating Model should I introduce first?
evals.expected_output: A prioritized guidance on which product operating model components to introduce first, with sequencing rationale
expectations: Output maps the challenge to the relevant system(s) in the operating model
Output recommends a starting sequence based on the described situation
Output identifies which specific skills address the described problems
Output provides practical sequencing advice for introducing the operating model
Output connects all recommended skills back to the operating model overview

evals.prompt: My team is asking which product skill to use for different situations. Give me a quick reference guide for when to use product-strategy vs product-discovery vs product-architecture vs product-delivery vs product-leadership.
evals.expected_output: A clear decision guide mapping situations to the appropriate product skill
expectations: Output provides a clear decision matrix or guide for each skill
Output gives specific example situations for each skill
Output explains how the skills connect and feed into each other
Output is quick-reference format (not a long narrative)
Output helps users select the right skill without reading all of them
